name: NIST CSF
description: NIST Cybersecurity Framework version 2.0 — a set of guidelines for mitigating organizational cybersecurity risks.

families.description: Develop an organizational understanding to manage cybersecurity risk to systems, people, assets, data, and capabilities.

controls.title: Physical devices and systems inventory
controls.description: Physical devices and systems within the organization are inventoried.

controls.title: Software platforms and applications inventory
controls.description: Software platforms and applications within the organization are inventoried.

controls.title: Asset vulnerabilities identification
controls.description: Asset vulnerabilities are identified and documented.

families.description: Develop and implement appropriate safeguards to ensure delivery of critical services.

controls.title: Identities and credentials management
controls.description: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited for authorized devices, users, and processes.

controls.title: Data-at-rest protection
controls.description: Data-at-rest is protected with appropriate safeguards.
